claimText: Nipah virus is a zoonotic pathogen transmitted from bats or infected animals to humans, causing severe respiratory and neurological illness.
evidenceExcerpt: Nipah virus is a zoonotic pathogen transmitted from bats or infected animals to humans, causing severe respiratory and neurological illness.
examUse: Statement-based question on the transmission and symptoms of Nipah virus.

claimText: Monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced proteins engineered to bind specific antigens, mimicking natural immune responses.
evidenceExcerpt: Monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced proteins engineered to bind specific antigens, mimicking natural immune responses.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and function of monoclonal antibodies.
distractorHooks: Naturally occurring proteins extracted from plants
Engineered to bind multiple random antigens simultaneously
Live attenuated viruses used for vaccination

claimText: Monoclonal antibodies are derived from identical immune cells cloned from a single parent B-cell.
evidenceExcerpt: Derived from identical immune cells cloned from a single parent B-cell.
examUse: Statement-based question on the cellular origin of monoclonal antibodies.
distractorHooks: Extracted directly from bone marrow without cloning

claimText: Monoclonal antibodies are used to treat cancers, autoimmune disorders, and viral infections.
evidenceExcerpt: mAbs are derived from identical immune cells cloned from a single parent B-cell and are used to treat cancers, autoimmune disorders, and viral infections.
examUse: Application-based question on the therapeutic uses of monoclonal antibodies.
distractorHooks: Cannot be used for autoimmune disorders
